$comment: Mendix entity model for the AI Interaction Framework's PromptTemplate library. This file describes the entities, attributes, and associations that a Mendix developer should create in the module 'AIInteractionFramework'. Template record JSON files in this library conform to this shape and can be imported via the ImportPromptTemplates microflow described in the design spec.

entities.name: PromptTemplate
entities.description: A versioned, governed prompt template. Immutable once Status = Published.

attributes.name: Code
attributes.description: Stable human-readable code, e.g. 'SCM-02-RFQ-Extractor'. Combined with Version forms the unique key.

attributes.name: Primitive
attributes.type: Enum[DocumentExtract, Classify, Answer, Draft, Cluster, Score, Summarize]

attributes.name: SystemPrompt
attributes.description: Role, invariants, and output contract. XML-tagged sections recommended.

attributes.name: UserPromptTemplate
attributes.description: User-turn template with {{variable}} placeholders. Rendered by the framework before invocation.

attributes.name: OutputSchemaJson
attributes.description: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) the completion must validate against.

attributes.name: MinConfidenceThreshold
attributes.description: If overall_confidence in the completion falls below this, mark invocation LowConfidence and route to review.

attributes.name: Temperature
attributes.description: Document-Extract templates should default to deterministic (0.0).

attributes.name: RequiresCitation
attributes.description: Document-Extract enforces citation on every extracted fact.

attributes.name: DataSensitivityTier
attributes.description: Framework uses this to select a compliant backend and apply routing policy.

attributes.name: SupersededByCode
attributes.description: On deprecation, points to the replacement template Code.

entities.name: PromptVariable
entities.description: A named input slot that the UserPromptTemplate references as {{name}}. The framework validates inputs against these variables before invocation.

attributes.name: DataType
attributes.type: Enum[String, Integer, Decimal, Boolean, Json, DocumentText, CitationList]

attributes.name: MaxLength
attributes.description: Optional cap for DocumentText to protect the context window.

entities.name: FewShotExample
entities.description: An example (input, expected-output) pair inlined into the user prompt. Used sparingly — one or two per template.

attributes.name: InputJson
attributes.description: JSON object whose keys match PromptVariable.Name.

attributes.name: ExpectedOutputJson
attributes.description: JSON that validates against the template's OutputSchemaJson.

entities.name: Guardrail
entities.description: A declarative policy attached to a template. The framework runtime enforces these on the invocation (input filtering, output filtering, escalation).

attributes.name: Kind
attributes.type: Enum[InputRedaction, OutputRedaction, RequireCitation, SchemaValidation, ConfidenceThreshold, DataClassificationRoute, PromptInjectionDefense, RefuseIfUngrounded]

attributes.name: ConfigJson
attributes.description: Policy-specific JSON config, e.g. regex for redaction, confidence cutoff, etc.

entities.name: ExtractionInvocation
entities.description: One execution of a Document-Extract template. Immutable audit record.

attributes.name: InputPayloadJson
attributes.description: Variable values used to render the user prompt (documents may be referenced by ID instead of inlined for payload size).

entities.name: Citation
entities.description: A per-field pointer from an extracted value back to its source document location. Produced by the framework after parsing the completion.

attributes.name: FieldPath
attributes.description: JSON Pointer to the field in OutputPayloadJson, e.g. '/clauses/0/far'.

notes: Publish is irreversible for a given (Code, Version). Corrections require a new Version.
ExtractionInvocation and Citation are append-only to protect the audit trail.
The framework exposes a microflow IF_DocumentExtract_Invoke(template_code, template_version_or_latest, input_object) -> ExtractionInvocation. See Appendix A of the design spec for the full flow.
